title: Live collab uses per-user op feeds plus ACT-gated collab manifest
content:
## Shape of live collaboration

- **Drive-level first, doc-level second.** Start with "collaborate on this whole drive" -- covers `ADD_FOLDER`, `MOVE_NODE`, and per-doc ops. Narrow to doc-level in a second pass using a tighter ACT grantee chain.
- **Per-user op feeds + collab manifest.** Each collaborator writes ops to their own feed, because the Swarm constraint is that only the owner can write. A **collaboration manifest**, ACT-gated, lists participants and targets. Each collaborator reads every other collaborator's feeds.
- **ACT gates read access.** Removing a participant rebuilds the ACT chain via `patchGrantees`.
- **GSOC `op-committed` pings** replace the polling timer for active collabs. Debounced ~300 ms so a burst of ops triggers one pull, not one per op.
- **Signatures already preserved** end-to-end (shipped 2026-04-17), so toolbar history shows the real author of every revision.

## Build order

1. Adapter plumbing
2. Collab manifest + ACT
3. SwarmChannel peer-feed registration
4. GSOC pings
5. Collaborate tab
6. Invitation card
7. Toolbar history auto-refresh
8. Doc-level scope

See `live-collaboration-design.md` for detail.
description: Live collab starts drive-level, then doc-level. Each collaborator writes to their own feed; an ACT-gated manifest lists participants; GSOC pings replace polling.
